description: Hypericum androsaemum, the shrubby St. John's wort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae. It is commonly called tutsan or sweet-amber. It forms a shrub with oval-shaped leaves and yellow flowers.
Hypericum ascyron, the great St. Johnswort or giant St. John's wort is a flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae.
Three to five celled capsule, sometimes fleshy.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Hypericum elodes, commonly known as marsh St John's-wort, is a species of flowering plant in the St. John's wort family Hypericaceae. It is native to Western Europe.
Hypericum hirsutum is a species of flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae, commonly known as hairy St John's-wort. It is found in Western Europe.
Hypericum humifusum is a prostrate flowering plant in the St. John's wort family Hypericaceae commonly known as trailing St John's-wort. It is found in Western Europe.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Hypericum maculatum, commonly known as imperforate St John's-wort, or spotted St. Johnswort, is a species of perennial herbaceous flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae. It is native to Europe and Western Asia where it grows in moist meadows.
Hypericum montanum is a plant species in the genus Hypericum commonly known as pale St. John's-wort or mountain St. John's wort. It is native to Eurasia and Morocco in North Africa.

Hypericum perforatum, commonly known as St. John's wort, is a flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae. It is a hairless, perennial herb with woody roots, yellow flowers marked by black glands, and leaves that appear perforated due to translucent glands, producing thousands of seeds per plant.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: A (2)3-5 celled ovoid 1/2 inch capsule, sometimes fleshy. Displays from July to November.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Hypericum pulchrum is a flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae, commonly known as slender St John's-wort. It is native to Western Europe.

Hypericum tetrapterum is a herbaceous perennial plant species in the flowering plant family Hypericaceae. Its common names include St. Peter's wort, Peterwort, square stemmed St. John's wort, and square stalked St. John's wort.
